scope: M10-relevant project-side .lean files (excludes lean/.lake/ and GoldbachHelfgott/)

lakefile_targets.GoldbachHelfgott: lean_lib with roots = [Basic, Statement, Reduction, Certificate, Main] (out of 141C scope)

lakefile_orphans: proof_targets.lean (not declared in any lean_lib root in lakefile.lean; relies on default-target globbing or explicit invocation)
CardEvenOfInvolution.lean (not declared in any lean_lib root in lakefile.lean; appears to be staging file)
unused_imports_in_node_set: WallisFamily.lean: imports CasoratianPi4 and ShiftConsistency; CasoratianPi4 helpers (raw_casoratian_step, reduced_casoratian_step, casoratian_closed_form, pi4_series_of_bridge) are NOT referenced by name in WallisFamily.lean's visible body, suggesting CasoratianPi4 import may be dead at HEAD
missing_imports_relative_to_blockers: B4 (`nhds` notation): no missing import; `import Mathlib` (L1) is broad enough; the issue is open-scope ordering not import absence
B2 (intertwining_lemma): `Solves` is reachable via WallisFamily.ShiftConsistency import; no missing import
notes: WallisFamily/LemmaK.lean is unreferenced by WallisFamily.lean and is only imported by proof_targets.lean. It is a leaf in the M10 graph and not on the critical build path of the WallisFamily lean_lib target. Build failures in WallisFamily.lean cannot be caused by LemmaK.lean. Conversely, proof_targets.lean depends on LemmaK.lean compiling, so a green WallisFamily build does not transitively imply a green proof_targets.lean.
